# @kernelui/react — LLM reference

A component library built on real semantic HTML instead of div soup: every
component wraps a genuine native element (`<button>`, `<dialog>`, `<input
type="color">`, `<select>`...) or a documented WAI-ARIA pattern where no
native element exists (Tabs, Accordion's `<details>`, the combobox
pattern). If you already know HTML and ARIA, you already know most of the
behavior this library gives you — read this file once, then trust the
exported TypeScript types (`dist/index.d.ts`) for exact prop names.

## Install

```bash
npm install @kernelui/react @kernelui/styles
```

```tsx
import "@kernelui/styles";        // tokens: color, radius, spacing, type
import "@kernelui/react/styles.css"; // this package's compiled component CSS

import { Button, TextField } from "@kernelui/react";
```

Both CSS imports are required for the library's actual visual design.
Skip `@kernelui/react/styles.css` and every component still renders as its
real underlying element, fully functional, just unstyled — never broken.

## Conventions (true across nearly every component — learn these once)

- **Controlled/uncontrolled value**: `value`/`defaultValue`/`onValueChange`
  (not `onChange`, which is reserved for the native DOM event where a
  component also exposes one). Omit `value` and the component manages its
  own state; pass `value` + `onValueChange` to fully control it.
- **Form-field scaffold** (`TextField`, `Textarea`, `Select`, `Combobox`,
  `TagInput`, `ColorPicker`, `FileUpload`, `NumberField`, `Slider`):
  `label` (required — the accessible name, always rendered as a real
  `<label>`), `hideLabel` (boolean — visually hides the label without
  removing it from the accessibility tree; `label` is still required when
  this is set), `description` (helper text), `errorMessage` (shown
  instead of `description`, with `role="alert"`, when `invalid` is true),
  `invalid` (boolean), `disabled`, `required`.
- **Styling hooks**: `className` (on the actual interactive element,
  accepts a string or `(state) => string` for state-driven styling) and
  `wrapperClassName` (on the outer wrapper, same shape) where a component
  has both. Every component also forwards a `ref` to its real underlying
  DOM element via `forwardRef` — never a wrapper div.
- **Real elements, not ARIA-only**: don't add `role`/`aria-*` props that
  duplicate what the underlying native element already provides (e.g.
  don't add `role="button"` to `<Button>` — it's a real `<button>`).
- **No hidden global config**: every component takes its data via props,
  not a Provider you have to wrap the app in — the exceptions are
  `<ToastViewport>` (mount once, then call the imported `toast()`
  function from anywhere) and `<CommandPalette>` (same idea).
- **React and Web Components share one design**: `@kernelui/elements`
  ships every component below as a framework-free Custom Element
  (`<kernel-button>`, `<kernel-text-field>`, ...) with the same visual
  design and equivalent kebab-case attributes (`onValueChange` becomes a
  `change` event with `event.detail`, boolean props become boolean
  attributes). Reach for it in Vue/Svelte/Astro/plain-HTML projects
  instead of this package.

## Components

### Primitives
- **Avatar** — Real image, with an initials fallback.
- **Badge** — A small status chip.
- **Button** — Click, keyboard, and form association, for free.
- **Checkbox** — Tri-state, styled with `:checked` and `:indeterminate`.
- **Label** — A standalone label for custom composed controls.
- **RadioGroup** (+ `RadioGroupItem`) — Grouped choice with native arrow-key roving.
- **Separator** — A divider that's already `role="separator"`.
- **Switch** — The WAI-ARIA switch pattern on a real button.
- **Toggle** — A two-state toggle that looks and acts like a button.

### Forms
- **ColorPicker** — A real colour input, plus a typeable hex field on the same value.
- **Combobox** — Filterable list with `aria-activedescendant`.
- **FileUpload** — Drag-and-drop over a real file input, never a replacement for it.
- **InputOTP** — One-time-code entry with roving focus.
- **NumberField** — `TextField`'s scaffold, specialised for numbers, with stepper buttons.
- **Select** (+ `SelectOption`) — The platform's own picker UI, on every device.
- **Slider** — Custom-painted thumb, native drag and keyboard behaviour.
- **TagInput** — Freeform text that commits into discrete, removable tags.
- **TextField** — Correct labelling and live validation styling.
- **Textarea** — Auto-growing with `field-sizing: content`.

### Layout
- **Card** (+ `CardHeader`/`CardTitle`/`CardDescription`/`CardContent`/`CardFooter`) — For genuinely self-contained, distributable content.
- **Footer** — The `contentinfo` landmark.
- **Header** — The `banner` landmark.
- **Nav** (+ `NavLink`) — Active state via `aria-current`, no client JS.
- **Resizable** — Pane-to-pane split layouts.
- **ScrollArea** — Custom scrollbar styling as enhancement only.
- **Sidebar** — The complementary landmark.

### Feedback
- **Callout** — Live-region feedback that announces itself (`role="alert"`/`role="status"`).
- **Progress** — Reports its own value to assistive tech, natively.
- **Skeleton** — Loading placeholder tied to real content dimensions.
- **ToastViewport** + `toast()` — A shared, queued live region; mount once, call `toast()` from anywhere.

### Overlays
- **CommandPalette** — A searchable, keyboard-driven action list.
- **ContextMenu** — Right-click menu, same primitives as DropdownMenu.
- **Dialog** — `showModal()`: focus trap, top layer, and `::backdrop`, free.
- **DropdownMenu** (+ `MenuItem`/`MenuSeparator`) — Anchor-positioned menu with arrow-key navigation.
- **HoverCard** — Rich hover preview, a heavier Tooltip.
- **NavigationMenu** (+ subparts) — Multi-level site navigation, mega-menu via popover.
- **Popover** — Anchor-positioned floating panel.
- **Tooltip** — Hover/focus hints via the Popover API.

### Navigation
- **Breadcrumbs** (+ `BreadcrumbItem`/`BreadcrumbsTrail`) — Ordered trail, `aria-current` for the current page.
- **Pagination** (+ subparts) — Ordered page links, `aria-current` for the active one.
- **Tabs** (+ `TabsList`/`Tab`/`TabPanel`) — No native element exists yet, ARIA APG pattern.

### Data Display
- **Accordion** (+ `AccordionItem`) — Native disclosure, exclusive open via a shared name.
- **Carousel** (+ `CarouselSlide`) — Native scrolling instead of a JS carousel engine.
- **DataTable** — Table plus sort/filter/paginate behaviour layered on top.
- **DatePicker** — Calendar grid with `aria-selected`.
- **DateRangePicker** — Two-click range selection over the same calendar grid.
- **Table** (+ `TableCaption`/`TableHeader`/`TableBody`/`TableRow`/`TableHead`/`TableCell`) — Sortable headers, real `<caption>`, real `<th scope>`.

### AI
- **Composer** — A chat message input, with a live "thinking" state.
- **Reasoning** — A collapsible AI reasoning trace that auto-opens while streaming.
- **ThinkingIndicator** — The universal typing-indicator dots, next to real visible text.

## Where to look for exact prop types

This file is a map, not the source of truth. For exact prop names,
types, and defaults: read `dist/index.d.ts` in this package (every prop
has a JSDoc comment explaining the *why*, not just the type) — or, if
this package was installed alongside the project's docs site, every
component has a live Playground and a machine-readable markdown mirror
at `/components/<slug>.md` (and one combined `/llms-full.txt` for the
whole library) generated from the same pages a human would read.
